title: DECISION: Incremental re-indexing lives in the daemon (RAM writable index), gated on warm path
description: Because view() is read-only (see storage-split decision), incremental updates (remove stale function vectors + add changed ones, instead of the full ~30min re-embed) REQUIRE a RAM-resident writable usearch Index. DECISION: that index lives in the DAEMON (TLDR-atc). The daemon holds the writable Index, watches file mtimes, applies per-function deltas, and periodically save()s to disk. Cold CLI invocations use read-only view() of the last saved file. CONSEQUENCE: incremental indexing is NOT a property of usearch alone — it is unlocked by usearch + the daemon warm path together. This elevates TLDR-atc from a latency win to the keystone for incremental indexing.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: Add daemon warm path for semantic search (no semantic route exists — every query is cold)
description: DISCOVERED 2026-06-01 via daemon route scan + tldr explain. tldr-daemon exposes 21 routes (ping/status/tree/structure/extract/imports/calls/impact/dead/importers/arch/cfg/dfg/slice/complexity/search[BM25]/context/smells/maintainability/secrets/vuln) and ZERO semantic/similar/embed routes. crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/semantic.rs has 0 daemon references. CONSEQUENCE: every 'tldr semantic'/'similar'/'embed' invocation runs COLD in a fresh process -> SemanticIndex::build -> EmbeddingCache::open -> load_with_lock (cache.rs:236) deserializes the full ~1.1GB cache each time (RSS ~8GB), reloads the 110MB ONNX model (~0.5s), then embeds the query (~0.2s). For warm/interactive use this is a BIGGER lever than usearch: usearch view() removes the 4.3s load, but a daemon warm path removes the load AND the 0.5s model load AND keeps the index resident -> ac0 estimated ~230ms warm. SCOPE: add /semantic (+/similar,/embed) daemon route + handler that holds a warm SemanticIndex (mmap'd vectors once usearch lands) + a warm Embedder (model loaded once) in DaemonState, keyed by project+model+mtimes like other warm caches. Pairs with TLDR-ac0 (usearch storage) and TLDR-h27/y0q (CLI correctness).
acceptance_criteria: tldr semantic/similar/embed route through the daemon when running; warm query latency ~200-300ms (no per-query 1.1GB load, no per-query model reload); cold fallback preserved when daemon absent; results identical to cold path.
notes: UNLOCKS INCREMENTAL INDEXING (research 2026-06-01): usearch view()'d mmap index is READ-ONLY (issue#97 — cannot add to disk). Incremental updates (remove stale keys + add changed-function vectors, avoiding the full ~30min re-embed) REQUIRE a RAM-resident writable index. The daemon warm path is exactly that home: hold the writable usearch Index in DaemonState, apply deltas on file-mtime change, periodically save(). So this P0 is not just a latency win — it's the prerequisite for incremental re-indexing. Cold CLI keeps using read-only view().

title: Default 0.50 similarity threshold miscalibrated for query-prefix regime (hides correct results)
description: DISCOVERED while dogfooding tldr semantic on our own codebase (TLDR-blm work). The Arctic query prefix (TLDR-dlk, default-ON) improves RANKING decisively (eval recall@5 0.071->0.857) but LOWERS absolute cosine scores, because the asymmetric prefix puts query embeddings in a different region than raw-code passages (query.passage cosine < passage.passage cosine). The user-facing 'tldr semantic' applies a fixed default --threshold 0.50. Result: the CORRECT answer for 'cosine similarity between two embedding vectors' is cosine_similarity() but it scores 0.46 -> filtered out -> CLI prints 'No matches found above threshold'. So the feature looks BROKEN to a user despite the recall win. The eval harness bypasses the threshold (pure ranking) which is why it reported 0.857. FIX OPTIONS: (a) lower/remove the default threshold and rely on top-k ranking; (b) recalibrate threshold for the prefixed-query score distribution; (c) make threshold prefix-aware. Repro: tldr semantic 'cosine similarity between two embedding vectors' crates/tldr-core/src/semantic --top 3  (default threshold -> no matches; --threshold 0.0 -> cosine_similarity appears at rank 3, score 0.46).
acceptance_criteria: tldr semantic with default settings surfaces the correct top-ranked result for the gold queries (not filtered by threshold); eval-measured ranking and CLI-visible results agree.
notes: Child of TLDR-blm epic. Surfaced by the prefix verdict; a user-facing gap between eval-measured ranking (good) and CLI-visible results (threshold-suppressed).
close_reason: Default semantic threshold 0.5->0.0 (rank top-N, no cutoff) so the prefix-regime scores (~0.46) are no longer hidden; also fixed the formatter's hardcoded 0.50 display to show the actual threshold. PROVEN: 'tldr semantic <q>' with default settings now returns cosine_similarity instead of 'No matches'. semantic.rs.

title: Cache key strip_prefix silently falls back to raw path (can revert the absolute-vs-relative bug)
description: Codex review (2026-06-02, thread a708b3161b2e63d89). CacheKey::from_chunk (crates/tldr-core/src/semantic/cache.rs) strips the build root via strip_prefix and on FAILURE falls back to chunk.file_path.to_string_lossy() VERBATIM (the raw as-given path). For a symlinked root, a differently-normalized path, or a chunk outside the root, this silently re-creates the original absolute-vs-relative key-divergence (the TLDR-atc bug -> re-embed). walker.rs does NOT canonicalize (stores root.as_ref()) and the chunker stores path.to_path_buf(), so the 'all chunk paths are root-prefixed' assumption is unverified at the type level. FIX: canonicalize the root once + derive file_rel_path deterministically; on strip_prefix miss, log a WARNING and use a single canonical fallback (canonical absolute path), never the raw path. Same rule applies to the future usearch keys (l5d). Introduced with TLDR-atc cache-key fix (401a7ac).

title: Daemon handle_notify takes the semantic-index sync Mutex on the async thread (blocks runtime)
description: Codex review (2026-06-02, thread a708b3161b2e63d89). handle_notify (crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/daemon.rs) acquires the semantic_index Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>>> DIRECTLY in async code to invalidate it. The Semantic handler holds that same mutex across the whole build+search INSIDE spawn_blocking (correct). But a Notify arriving mid-build calls .lock() on the async thread and PARKS a Tokio worker for the entire build (seconds), defeating the spawn_blocking responsiveness fix (commit 7199f03). FIX: Notify must not lock the index on the async path — signal invalidation via an AtomicBool/mpsc channel (or do the lock inside spawn_blocking), keeping handle_notify O(1)/lock-free. See docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md section 8. Introduced with TLDR-atc spawn_blocking work.

title: Expand semantic eval gold set 14 -> 50-100 cases (single-case swings make all deltas untrustworthy)
description: The semantic_eval harness has 14 gold (query->file/fn) cases. At n=14, one case = 0.071 recall, so model/feature comparisons swing on a SINGLE query: e.g. ArcticL-vs-ArcticM looked like 'L is worse @5' but the entire difference was ONE query (parse imports rank 1->8) — i.e. within noise. This makes it impossible to trust the 4er-hybrid lift, the 9m2-descriptions lift, or any model choice. FIX: grow the gold set to 50-100 query->target pairs spanning the static layers (ast/cfg/dfg/callgraph), search, and semantic subsystems, with a mix of easy (lexically-obvious) and hard (modality-gap) queries, so recall@5/@10/MRR deltas become statistically meaningful. Gate trustworthy measurement of TLDR-4er and TLDR-9m2 on this.
acceptance_criteria: Gold set >=50 cases; a single-query rank change moves recall by <0.02; 4er/9m2 lifts measured against the expanded set with the deltas exceeding single-case noise.

title: Incremental re-indexing: delta add/remove on file change instead of full re-embed
description: Today every index build re-chunks the whole tree and re-embeds anything whose content-hash changed; a cold full embed is ~30min. usearch supports remove(key)/add(key)/rename on a writable RAM index, enabling INCREMENTAL updates: on a file's mtime change, re-chunk only that file, remove its stale function-keys, add the changed ones. Turns a 30-min rebuild into a few-ms-per-file delta. REQUIRES a RAM-resident writable index (view() is read-only, issue#97) -> lives in the daemon (TLDR-atc). Key scheme: stable u64 key per function identity (path+name+signature hash) so deltas target the right vectors. Persist via periodic save().
acceptance_criteria: Editing one file re-embeds only that file's changed functions (not the corpus); deleted functions' vectors are removed; query results reflect the change within one daemon save cycle; full rebuild still available as fallback.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: Design doc written: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (committed 73cf41f). Covers stable u64 chunk keys + metadata sidecar + per-file key index, the embed/meta-only/remove delta classifier, debounce/coalesce pipeline w/ burst cap, atomic save + startup reconcile, spawn_blocking+Mutex concurrency, edge cases, full-rebuild fallback, 4-phase rollout (P0 usearch store=l5d -> P1 delta core -> P2 debounce -> P3 persistence). Gated on l5d (usearch writable RAM index; view() read-only NOT used at our scale).

title: DECISION: Storage access split — read-only view() for queries, load()+RAM for mutation
description: FINDING (usearch issue #97, maintainer): a view()'d memory-mapped on-disk index is READ-ONLY — you cannot add() to it (the future 'merge' feature aside). DECISION: COLD CLI query path uses view() (mmap, low RSS, fast, no full load). UPDATE/WRITE path uses load() into a writable RAM-resident Index, then add/remove/save(). These are two distinct access modes over the same .usearch file. Consequence: incremental updates cannot happen against the mmap'd file directly — they need the RAM index (see daemon decision).
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: DECISION: Search algorithm = exact brute-force (exact_search_f32), HNSW deliberately NOT used
description: CONTEXT: usearch's headline feature is HNSW approximate ANN. CHOSEN: use exact_search_f32 (brute force), NOT search_f32 (HNSW). RATIONALE: usearch docs say HNSW is for 'millions of entries'; exact is recommended for 'smaller collections'. tldr targets <=100K LOC = 14K-117K vectors. Exact gives 100% recall (vs HNSW 99.3%), and the scan was never the bottleneck (0.1ms; the LOAD was). add() still builds an HNSW graph (~1.5s @117K, negligible vs 30min embed) but we never query it — acceptable; optionally set low connectivity to trim graph RAM. REVISIT only if corpus scale reaches ~1M+ vectors. This is vendor-endorsed config, not a limitation.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: DECISION: Vector store backend = usearch (exact mode), not hand-rolled mmap
description: CONTEXT: semantic search's JSON cache (1.1GB, 4.3s cold parse on EVERY query — no daemon route) needed replacing. Two candidates: plain memmap2+bincode (pure Rust) vs usearch (cxx->C++). CHOSEN: usearch. RATIONALE: usearch view() = mmap load (kills 4.3s), exact_search_f32 = exact brute-force (100% recall, 20x faster than FAISS flat), save/load = binary persistence, ScalarKind = optional quantization — i.e. it delivers the exact goal as a MAINTAINED library, collapsing ~8 hand-rolled children into one integration. The earlier anti-usearch premise ('HNSW is approximate, we need exact') was FALSE: exact_search exists. COST ACCEPTED: C++ compiler in build/CI (cxx bridge). Supersedes TLDR-7kf/8pt/k4q/j8c. See memory usearch-discovery / decision-locked.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: Eval harness benchmarks ArcticM (code default) but users run their configured model (e.g. ArcticL) — benchmark doesn't measure what's deployed
description: The semantic_eval harness builds its index with Some(Default::default()) (semantic_eval.rs:121) -> EmbedOptions::default() -> EmbeddingModel::default() = ArcticM (768-dim, the #[default] enum variant). It NEVER reads ~/.tldr/config.json. The real CLI path uses EmbeddingModel::resolve(config) which DOES read config. On this machine the global config sets model=arctic-l (1024-dim), so the user's actual 'tldr semantic' runs ArcticL while the benchmark runs ArcticM. CONSEQUENCE: the prefix verdict (recall@5 0.071->0.857, MRR 0.685) is measured on ArcticM and is UNVALIDATED for the model the user actually deploys. The handoff's claim 'benchmark with the PRODUCTION model ArcticM' was imprecise — ArcticM is the shipped enum default, not necessarily the user's configured/production model. There is no beads decision establishing ArcticM as 'production' (searched). FIX: make the eval resolve the model the same way the CLI does (EmbeddingModel::resolve via TldrConfig::resolve), OR accept a model arg/env so the benchmark can target the deployed model. Then re-run the prefix verdict on ArcticL to validate. Also revisit which model is the intended default/production target — separate product decision.
acceptance_criteria: Eval harness benchmarks the same model the CLI resolves (config-aware or explicit arg); prefix verdict re-confirmed on ArcticL; intended production/default model documented.
notes: Child of TLDR-blm. Measurement-validity gap surfaced during the prefix-verdict dogfooding. Pairs with TLDR-h27 (threshold) — both are 'eval passes but CLI behaves differently' gaps: eval hardcodes ArcticM + threshold 0.0, CLI uses ArcticL + threshold 0.50.
close_reason: semantic_eval now resolves the deployed model via TldrConfig::resolve (matches the CLI), with TLDR_EVAL_MODEL override, instead of the hardcoded EmbeddingModel::default() (ArcticM). Eval now benchmarks the model users actually run (e.g. ArcticL). semantic_eval.rs.

title: LLM-generated function descriptions for embedding (summary-augmented retrieval)
description: Highest-leverage quality lever for semantic search. At index time, call a cheap/fast LLM (e.g. Haiku) per chunk to generate a 1-line natural-language description of what the function does, then embed THAT (alongside or instead of code). Attacks the root cause: queries are English but raw code lacks query words; an English description bridges the gap (e.g. 'combines two ranked lists via reciprocal rank fusion' matches a 'combine two rankings' query). Known RAG pattern (document/summary augmentation; cousin of HyDE). NEITHER llm-tldr (python) NOR tldr-core has attempted this (verified by grep). User will provide LLM API key.
design: Indexing stage: (1) per-chunk LLM call -> 1-line description; (2) CACHE keyed by content_hash so we pay once per function and only re-summarize changed functions (mirror the embedding cache's incremental model); (3) feed description into build_embedding_text. Cost: ~one cheap call per function, one-time + incremental, fully cached. Provider/key via existing config (config.rs already has embedding provider/api_key_env scaffolding). DEFERRED to a later iteration per user; this task captures it.
acceptance_criteria: Per-chunk descriptions generated via configurable LLM provider; cached by content hash; included in embedding text; measurable recall@5/MRR lift vs the prefix+enrichment baseline on the eval harness.
notes: SEQUENCING (2026-06-01): TLDR-d8f (embed EXISTING docstrings, free, no API key) is the cheaper precursor — do it first and measure. 9m2's LLM-generated descriptions then target the functions that LACK docstrings (or to augment thin ones). Both attack the same modality gap; d8f is the zero-cost slice, 9m2 the full-coverage version. Still needs your LLM API key (Haiku per spec).

title: Phase 1b: set Arctic query/passage prefix in embed + search
description: EmbedOptions::use_prefix (embedder.rs:57) exists but is never set. Arctic models need asymmetric prefixes: passage prefix for documents (build), query prefix for the search query (index.rs:375). Free recall win. Validate exact prefix strings against fastembed/Arctic docs.
close_reason: Arctic query prefix shipped in e9e5427 (embedder.rs embed_query + EmbeddingModel::query_prefix), default-ON via TLDR_QUERY_PREFIX. Full-scale eval verdict: recall@5 0.071->0.857, MRR 0.083->0.685 on crates/tldr-core/src. Decisive win, stays default-on.

title: Phase 1a: wire enrichment.rs into SemanticIndex::build
description: enrichment.rs (build_embedding_text, 8-field recipe matching the Python original) is fully implemented but NEVER called. index.rs:310-313 embeds raw chunk.content. Wire enrich_chunks+build_embedding_text into the build path. MUST bump cache key with an embed-recipe version tag (e.g. embed_schema=enriched-v1) so stale raw-embedded vectors aren't reused. Biggest quality lever; no new dependency.
notes:
QUADRATIC FIXED: enrichment re-parsed each file ~3x PER FUNCTION via get_cfg_context/get_dfg_context. Now FileAnalysisCache parses each file ONCE (file_trees) and CFG/DFG reuse it via extract_cfg_from_tree + extract_dfg_from_tree_with_cfg (the pre-parsed helpers that already existed for vuln scanning). Result: enrich-on over 272 chunks = 16.7s (was: hung 12min/12GB on full crate). 

MEASURED (semantic/ scope, 8 cases, prefix on): enrich OFF -> R@5 0.875/MRR 0.875; enrich ON (with CFG/DFG) -> R@5 0.875/MRR 0.708. Enrichment did NOT help here — slightly hurt ranking. Tempers the 'enrichment is the biggest lever' hypothesis. CAVEATS: small/noisy scope; CFG/DFG-on variant only (cheap signal-only untested, blocked by cache tag not capturing flow flag); full-corpus untested. Enrichment stays GATED OFF by default. Prefix remains the only proven win so far.
close_reason: Enrichment wired into SemanticIndex::build + per-function re-parse quadratic fixed (reuses extract_cfg_from_tree/extract_dfg_from_tree_with_cfg), shipped 37ab719+e9e5427. Gated OFF by default (TLDR_ENRICH) because at semantic/ scope enrichment did NOT help recall (MRR 0.875->0.708 w/ CFG/DFG). Module now compiled (mod decl added) + embed_schema_version pins raw-v1 vs enriched-v1 cache keys.

title: Phase 0: semantic search eval harness (recall@5 / MRR gold set)
description: Hand-author ~12-15 (query -> expected file+function) cases over THIS codebase and compute recall@5 + MRR, so semantic-search quality is measurable before/after Phase 1 changes. Drives existing SemanticIndex::build+search. Capture baseline before any change.
notes: BASELINE (crates/tldr-core/src, ArcticM, raw-source embeddings, no query prefix, no hybrid): Recall@5=0.143 (2/14), Recall@10=0.143, MRR=0.086. Only 'generate embeddings for a batch' (rank 1) and 'reaching definitions' (rank 5) hit. Even 'cosine similarity' misses top-10. Empirically confirms search is broken. Harness: crates/tldr-core/examples/semantic_eval.rs (cargo run -p tldr-core --release --example semantic_eval).
close_reason: Eval harness built + baseline captured (R@5=0.143, MRR=0.086). Measurement vehicle for Phase 1.

title: Results-equivalence verification harness
description: The real pass/fail gate. Replay the captured baseline queries (tests 1-7 outputs: scores + snippets, the golden reference) against the new mmap-backed build and DIFF the ranked results. With brute-force + f32 kept, results must be IDENTICAL. Also capture cold-latency old vs new to confirm the ~710ms single-file target. Latency improvement is necessary but NOT sufficient -- equivalence is the gate. Record results on the epic.
notes: REFRAMED: verify usearch exact_search_f32 returns results IDENTICAL to the current brute-force top_k_similar over the captured baseline (scores+order). This is the PASS/FAIL gate (results-equivalence, not latency) — usearch exact mode must match, else investigate metric/normalization (cosine vs IP; vectors are unit-normalized already).

title: Rewire EmbeddingCache onto usearch Index
description: Replace the JSON HashMap internals of EmbeddingCache (cache.rs) with the MmapVectorStore while keeping the PUBLIC API stable (open/get/put/flush/len/is_empty) so index.rs and similarity.rs need ZERO changes. get(chunk, model) -> hash -> store.get. put(chunk, embedding, model) -> store.add. Drop cached_at/file_mtime if reachability-GC decision lands. Verify index.rs build()/search()/find_similar() compile and behave unchanged.
notes: REFRAMED mmap->usearch. EmbeddingCache.get/put/flush delegate to the usearch wrapper (TLDR-l5d) instead of the JSON HashMap (cache.rs load_with_lock:236). Keep the get/put/flush signatures so callers (index.rs) are untouched.

title: Integrate usearch as vector store (add_f32 / exact_search_f32 / save / load) + key->metadata sidecar
description:
FIRST PASS = full-rebuild usearch store (NO incremental; incremental = TLDR-t8f). Plan per docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md sections 3-4.

SUPERSEDES the earlier hand-rolled memmap2+bincode 'MmapVectorStore' plan (TLDR-j8c): we use the usearch LIBRARY, not a hand-rolled mmap.

Store = key(u64) -> vector via usearch NativeIndex:
- add_f32(key, &[f32]) on build. f32 FIRST; i8 ScalarKind quantization is opt-in later (TLDR-ccg).
- exact_search_f32(query, count) for search — exact KNN (NOT HNSW); vendor-recommended for <1M vectors, ~20x faster than FAISS flat, 100% recall.
- save(path) / load(path). Use load() into a WRITABLE RAM index — NOT view(). view() is read-only (issue#97) and unnecessary at our scale (~58MB f32 / ~15MB i8): load() into RAM is cheap and keeps the index writable, which is the prerequisite for t8f incremental.
- metadata SIDECAR (separate file): key -> {identity, file_rel_path, function_name, class_name, line_start, line_end, content_hash}, plus a per-file key index. usearch stores ONLY vectors; everything else lives here.
- Metric = Cos (vectors are unit-normalized; IP also valid).
- Atomic save (unique temp + rename — reuse the cache-flush race fix from TLDR-atc). Version byte; graceful corruption/version-mismatch -> full rebuild.

Outcome vs the 1.4GB JSON cache.json: ~58MB f32 binary + small sidecar; daemon RAM ~2.3GB -> ~0.5GB; first-load ~14s -> sub-second.
acceptance_criteria: usearch-backed store builds the full index via add_f32 and searches via exact_search_f32 with results IDENTICAL to the JSON-cache path on the n=52 eval; save()/load() round-trips; metadata sidecar resolves key->{path,fn,class,line range,content_hash} + per-file key lookup; first-load drops ~14s -> sub-second and daemon RSS drops materially (1.4GB JSON -> ~58MB f32); f32 ONLY (i8 deferred to TLDR-ccg); NO incremental this pass (full rebuild only; incremental = TLDR-t8f); cache.json path seam-switched/removed.
notes:
USEARCH FEATURE GUIDANCE (research 2026-06-01): CORE = view() [read-only mmap, query path] + exact_search_f32 [100% recall, 20x faster than FAISS flat, vendor-recommended for <1M vectors] + save() + add(). add() builds an unused HNSW graph (~1.5s @117K, negligible) — fine; optionally set low connectivity to trim graph RAM. KEY CONSTRAINT (issue#97): view() is READ-ONLY — you canNOT add to a mmap'd on-disk index. So: QUERY path = view()+exact_search (low RSS); UPDATE path = load() into RAM + add/remove + save(). EXPLOIT LATER: i8 ScalarKind quantization (near-lossless for cosine+unit-norm, 4x smaller) as opt-in compact mode; filtered_search_f32 predicate for scoped/path-filtered queries. Metric = Cos (vectors already unit-normalized — could also use IP since normalized).
2026-06-01 RESCOPED to match docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (committed 73cf41f): use usearch load() into a WRITABLE RAM index, NOT view()/mmap (view() is read-only issue#97 and unneeded at ~58MB f32/~15MB i8 — load() is cheap and keeps the index writable, the prereq for t8f). f32 FIRST; i8 quantization is opt-in later (ccg). Metadata SIDECAR is required (usearch stores vectors only). Full-rebuild only this pass. The OLD description (hand-rolled memmap2+bincode 'MmapVectorStore' via j8c) is OBSOLETE — the usearch library supersedes it. Prior USEARCH FEATURE GUIDANCE notes remain valid for the API.
2026-06-02 CODEX REVIEW (thread a708b3161b2e63d89) — KEEP the sidecar IN l5d; do NOT defer it to t8f (an earlier reconciliation idea — REJECTED). Rationale: a persisted usearch index is vectors-only, so after a daemon RESTART, search results (path/fn/line range/snippet) cannot be reconstructed without the sidecar, and re-chunking the whole tree on every restart is exactly the cost we remove. So l5d ships: usearch vector store + sidecar + MANIFEST. ADD to scope: (1) Store MANIFEST {format_version, generation, embedding_model, dimensions, metric, scalar_kind, embed_schema, root, chunk_count, checksum}; load() REJECTS->full-rebuild on any mismatch (model/dims/scalar/schema/root/format or index-vs-manifest count/dims or checksum or index-vs-sidecar generation). (2) PAIRED-ATOMIC save: usearch save() is NOT atomic temp+rename — write index+sidecar+manifest to temps, fsync, rename, with the MANIFEST rename as the single commit point (crash before it leaves the old trio intact). (3) HARDEN the root-relative key: strip_prefix failure must warn + use a canonical fallback, NOT the silent raw-path fallback that reverts to the original bug. CAVEATS to document (not blockers): load() is scale-bounded (revisit view() past ~20 daemons / 500MB+ monorepo); i8 is a REAL option (exact_search_i8 + separate quantized index + quantized query) deferred to ccg, not irrelevant. Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (317293b).

title: Define MmapVectorStore format + abstraction
description: Design the on-disk format and the Rust abstraction for the vector store. Format: header (magic, version, model name, dim, count) + contiguous f32 vectors region (fixed stride = dim*4, 4-byte aligned) + a hash->offset index map (bincode) at the tail. One file per embedding model (dim is fixed per model: arctic-s 384 / arctic-m 768 / arctic-l 1024). Key = 64-bit hash(content_hash + file_path + function_name) -- same identity as today's CacheKey minus model (model implied by file). Define a trait so the backend is swappable. Must support zero-copy &[f32] reads from the mmap.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by usearch decision (TLDR-rgc). usearch defines the on-disk index format + abstraction; no custom MmapVectorStore format to design. Residual (thin wrapper + metadata sidecar) folded into reframed TLDR-l5d.

title: Lock backend + GC decisions for mmap store
description: Finalize the two open design forks before implementation: (1) Backend = plain mmap (memmap2 + bincode hash->offset map) vs usearch. Leaning plain mmap: usearch builds HNSW on every add() (adds cost to the embed path, our 2nd bottleneck) for a search we don't use; pure-Rust avoids a C++ build dep; brute-force stays exact at any scale this tool targets. (2) GC = reachability-on-full-reindex (leaning; drops cached_at/file_mtime, entry becomes key->Vec<f32>) vs timestamp bookkeeping file. Output: a recorded decision (bd decision) that unblocks the impl children.
design: DECIDED: usearch. C++ build dep (cxx bridge) accepted by user 2026-06-01. usearch exact_search_f32 + view (mmap) + save/load + ScalarKind cover the exact-search + load + persistence + quant goals as a maintained library. GC fork: GC-by-reachability on full re-index (usearch index rebuilt from chunks; drops per-entry timestamps -> simplest, matches the rebuild model). Cascade recorded in memory 'usearch-discovery'/'decision-locked'.
close_reason: DECIDED: usearch (C++ dep accepted). The mmap-vs-usearch fork is resolved in favor of usearch — exact_search_f32 + view + save/load + ScalarKind. GC = reachability-on-full-reindex. Unblocks the (reframed) impl children.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DECISION EVIDENCE (2026-06-01): The mmap-vs-usearch fork was framed on a FALSE premise. This issue's description says usearch 'builds HNSW on every add() for a search we dont use' and implies approximate-only. Scanned usearch/rust/lib.rs: it has exact_search_f32 (L456) = EXACT brute-force, and view (L542) = mmap load. So usearch delivers ac0's EXACT goal (mmap-backed exact search) as a maintained library, collapsing the 8 ac0 impl children into one integration. The HNSW-build-on-add cost is negligible vs the ~30min CPU embed. The decision therefore reduces to a SINGLE axis: usearch adds a C++ compiler dependency (cxx bridge) to the build/CI; plain mmap stays pure-Rust. RECOMMENDATION: usearch, unless 'no C++ in build' is a hard ship constraint. Awaiting that one confirmation before recording the bd decision + superseding 7kf/8pt/k4q.

title: Replace JSON embedding cache with usearch-backed vector store (keep exact search)
description:
## EPIC: Kill the 4.3s cold-query load in semantic search

### The measured problem
Cold `tldr semantic/similar/embed` query breaks down as: 4,300ms JSON parse + 500ms model load + 200ms query embed + 0.1ms brute-force cosine. The 1.1GB cache.json (117,641 entries) is loaded IN FULL to serve as few as 28 lookups. The brute-force cosine (0.1ms) is NOT the bottleneck.

### The fix (spine)
mmap-backed vector store replaces the JSON load. KEEP exact brute-force top_k_similar (HNSW would be approximate AND only saves 0.1ms->0.5ms). KEEP f32 (f16/i8 reorder results). Metadata stays sourced from chunk_code() at search time -- NO sidecar needed (verified: cache stores only the vector + 2 timestamps).

### Target
- Single-file cold query: ~5.2s -> ~710ms (mmap kills the 4.3s load).
- Directory queries: still re-chunk the tree for metadata; that cost stays. Do NOT promise 710ms for dirs.
- Daemon warm path (separate work): ~230ms (removes the 500ms model load too).

### PASS/FAIL = results-equivalence, not latency
Captured baseline outputs (scores+snippets) are the golden reference. With brute-force + f32 kept, new results must be IDENTICAL to old. Latency improvement is necessary but not sufficient.

### Open decisions (under discussion -- see child TLDR-rgc)
1. Backend: plain mmap (memmap2 + bincode hash->offset map, pure Rust, leaning) vs usearch (C++ dep, HNSW we don't use, adds cost to embed path).
2. GC: GC-by-reachability on full re-index (leaning, drops timestamps) vs key->timestamp bookkeeping file.

Full design rationale in the Design field.
design:
## Corrected understanding (replaces earlier 'metadata sidecar' framing)

VERIFIED in code: the embedding cache (cache.rs) stores ONLY {embedding: Vec<f32>, cached_at, file_mtime}. ALL metadata (file_path, function_name, line_start/end, snippet, content) is rebuilt by chunk_code() at search time (index.rs:236, 272-295). So there is NO metadata to persist -> NO sidecar needed for correctness. Simpler than last turn's proposal.

## The real bottleneck (from measured baseline)

Cold query = 4.3s JSON parse + 0.5s model load + 0.2s query embed + ~0.1ms cosine. The brute-force cosine is 0.1ms = irrelevant. HNSW would turn 0.1ms into 0.5ms = solves a non-problem AND is approximate (recall < 100%). 

=> SPINE OF THE FIX: mmap replaces the 1.1GB JSON load. KEEP exact brute-force top_k_similar. KEEP f32 (not f16/i8 -- those reorder results).

## Design

Embedding store = content-addressed KV: key = 64-bit hash(content_hash + file_path + function_name); value = Vec<f32>. One store file per model (dim is fixed per model: arctic-s 384 / arctic-m 768 / arctic-l 1024).
- open(): mmap the file (~0ms) instead of serde_json::from_reader (4.3s).
- get(chunk, model): hash -> lookup -> &[f32]. 
- put/flush: append/rewrite binary.
- Search & find_similar: UNCHANGED. Still chunk_code() for metadata, still top_k_similar() exact cosine.

## Open decision: usearch vs plain mmap (memmap2 + bincode)
- We'd use ~5% of usearch (only mmap KV get; NOT HNSW). 
- Plain memmap2 + bincode(hash->offset) gets the IDENTICAL load win, no C++ toolchain dep, no HNSW graph-build cost on every embed.
- usearch justified ONLY by: tested serialization, headroom above 100K cap, future ANN if corpus grows. NOT by 'faster' (for this corpus it isn't).

## GC (TTL is lost -- usearch/binary store has no timestamps)
content_hash in key => changed content makes a new key, orphaning old. Pick ONE:
(a) GC-by-reachability: on full re-index, enumerate keys, drop any not re-added.
(b) tiny key->timestamp bookkeeping file for TTL/mtime eviction.

## Scope caveat (honesty)
730ms target is the SINGLE-FILE number. Directory searches (src/) still re-chunk the whole tree every run for metadata -- that cost stays and scales, mmap or not. Do NOT promise 730ms for directories. A sidecar that persists metadata (skip re-chunking on warm dir searches) is the ONLY place a sidecar earns its keep -- but we lack a warm-directory measurement (killed that run). Measure before promising.

## Verification = results-equivalence, not latency
Baseline outputs (scores+snippets) already captured are the golden reference. PASS = same queries old vs new produce IDENTICAL ranked results (exact, since brute-force + f32 kept) AND faster cold latency. Latency alone is NOT pass/fail.
notes:
## Baseline Measurements (JSON-based, release build)

Cache: ~/Library/Caches/tldr/embeddings/cache.json = 1.1 GB, 117,641 entries

### Completed Tests

| Test | Command | Wall time | Internal latency_ms | Result |
|------|---------|-----------|---------------------|--------|
| 1. Fresh embed (cached) | `tldr embed config.rs` | 4.36s | 4,393ms | 28 chunks, all cached |
| 2. Cached embed | `tldr embed config.rs` | 4.36s | 4,315ms | 28 chunks, all cached |
| 3. Semantic search (single file) | `tldr semantic "parse json config" config.rs` | 5.22s | 795ms | 10 results, cache_hit=false |
| 5. Cold search (single file) | `tldr semantic "error handling" config.rs` | 7.37s | 1,098ms | 10 results |
| 6. Config model check | `tldr embed config.rs` (config=arctic-l) | 4.41s | 4,370ms | model: arctic-l ✓ |
| 7. CLI flag override | `tldr embed config.rs --model arctic-s` | 4.41s | 4,378ms | model: arctic-s ✓ |
| 8. Existing tests | `cargo test semantic` | 60s (compile) / 6.88s (run) | — | 8 passed, 17 ignored |

### Key observation
Even for a CACHED 28-chunk file, embed takes 4.3s wall time. That's ~4.3s spent loading/parsing the 1.1GB JSON cache, NOT embedding. The actual search latency (795ms-1098ms) is on top of the cache parse.

### Pending Tests (running — first-time arctic-l embed of 14K chunks)
| Test | Command | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| 4. Similar function search | `tldr similar config.rs --function from_path` | Embedding 14,144 chunks (first time arctic-l) |
| 5b. Cold search (full src/) | `tldr semantic "error handling" src/` | Embedding full directory (first time arctic-l) |

These will complete and be recorded. The one-time embed cost is NOT part of the comparison — only the subsequent cached searches matter for the usearch benchmark.
2026-06-02 DECISION RESOLVED + reconciled with Codex review (thread a708b3161b2e63d89). (1) The 'usearch vs plain mmap (memmap2+bincode)' open decision is CLOSED -> usearch library (decision-locked memory: decision-locked-2026-06-01-vector-store-usearch). The hand-rolled memmap2 plan (and TLDR-j8c 'MmapVectorStore') is OBSOLETE. (2) Backend: usearch load() into a WRITABLE RAM index (NOT view()/mmap) — view() is read-only and at ~58MB f32 mmap earns nothing; load() keeps the index writable for deltas. load() is scale-bounded: revisit view() for the read path past ~20 daemons / 500MB+ monorepo. (3) SIDECAR REVISED: this epic previously said 'NO sidecar needed (metadata from re-chunk at search time)'. That holds for a COLD single-shot CLI, but NOT once the store is PERSISTED + served from a resident daemon (TLDR-atc, now done): a restart would have to re-chunk the whole tree to recover result metadata. So a metadata SIDECAR + MANIFEST is now a CORRECTNESS requirement of l5d, not optional. KEEP these still-valid caveats from the original epic text: exact brute-force (not HNSW); f32 first (i8=ccg, separate quantized index); single-file vs directory latency differ — do NOT promise 730ms for directory searches (re-chunk cost stays unless the sidecar persists metadata — which it now does). Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (317293b).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: PREMISE CORRECTION (2026-06-01): This epic chose hand-rolled plain-mmap partly on 'usearch=approximate/HNSW we dont use'. That is FALSE per usearch/rust/lib.rs: exact_search_f32 (L456) gives exact brute-force, view (L542) gives mmap load, ScalarKind gives optional quantization. usearch achieves this epic's core goal (kill the 4.3s JSON load via mmap, KEEP exact results) as a library — potentially obsoleting most children (j8c/l5d/m01/2a4/3gj/lhu/za0). Open question now narrows to: accept a C++ build dep (usearch) vs more pure-Rust custom code (mmap). See TLDR-rgc + memory 'usearch-discovery-2026-06-01'. If usearch is chosen, this epic's title/scope should be reframed from 'mmap store' to 'usearch-backed vector store'.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: BENEFIT-SIZING (2026-06-01, via tldr explain/structure + daemon route scan): The 4.3s JSON load is paid on EVERY semantic query, not once-per-daemon. CONFIRMED: tldr-daemon exposes 21 routes (ping/status/tree/structure/extract/imports/calls/impact/dead/importers/arch/cfg/dfg/slice/complexity/search[BM25]/context/smells/maintainability/secrets/vuln) and ZERO semantic/similar/embed routes — semantic.rs has 0 daemon references. So every 'tldr semantic' runs cold: SemanticIndex::build -> EmbeddingCache::open -> load_with_lock (cache.rs:236) deserializes the FULL ~1.1GB JSON into a HashMap each invocation (eval RSS ~8GB). The scan it does NOT help: top_k_similar (similarity.rs:238, cyclo 3, called by SemanticIndex.search:429/find_similar:533) is 0.1ms. usearch benefit = view() mmap kills the 4.3s load on every query + RSS GBs->working-set; exact_search_f32 keeps results identical. Floor after usearch ~0.7s (0.5s model load + 0.2s query embed remain — needs a daemon warm path, separate work). usearch does NOT change recall (still 0.857); it is purely load-time + memory + maintenance.

title: Wire resolved embedding model into Embedder (provider seam, local-only)
description: Make tldr semantic / tldr embed use the CONFIGURED model instead of the hardcoded default. Resolution order for model: -m flag > config embedding.model > built-in default (arctic-m). parse_model() already maps arctic-xs/s/m/m-long/l (semantic.rs:123) and to_fastembed_model() maps the enum (embedder.rs:187). Provider seam: if embedding.provider != "local", FAIL FAST at load with a clear message ('cloud embedding providers not supported in this build') — fields are still parsed so the file stays forward-valid. This delivers the core goal: per-project default model without recompiling. Tests: config model honored, -m flag overrides config, non-local provider errors cleanly.
close_reason: Added EmbeddingModel::parse() and EmbeddingModel::resolve() to tldr-core. resolve() implements precedence: CLI flag > config embedding.model > built-in default. Provider seam: fails fast with clear message when provider != local. Removed 3 duplicated parse_model() functions from embed.rs, semantic.rs, similar.rs; replaced with config-aware EmbeddingModel::resolve().

title: Config loader: precedence chain + per-field deep merge
description: Resolve effective config across layers. Load ~/.tldr/config.json (global, optional) then <project>/.tldr/config.json (project), deep-merge per field (project wins), then apply CLI-flag overrides (flag wins) as the final layer. Missing file at any layer = no-op. Project root resolution should match existing tldr conventions (the dir containing .tldr / .git / manifest). Output: a single resolved config struct the commands consume. Tests: global-only, project-overrides-global, CLI-overrides-both, all-absent = built-in defaults.
close_reason: Added merge() method for deep per-field merge (project wins over global), resolve() method that loads ~/.tldr/config.json then <project>/.tldr/config.json with deep merge. 5 tests: project-overrides-global, preserves-unset-fields, semantic-disabled-wins, all-absent-defaults, no-project-root.

title: Define TldrConfig serde structs + tolerant JSON parsing
close_reason: Added TldrConfig, EmbeddingConfig, SemanticConfig serde structs in tldr-core/src/config.rs with serde(default) on all fields, no deny_unknown_fields. from_str + from_path (returns default on missing file). 7 unit tests: empty {}, partial, unknown-keys-ignored, full cloud-shaped, missing file, malformed JSON, semantic disabled.

title: Per-project config file (.tldr/config.json) for embedding model + settings
description:
Goal: let users change the embedding model (and future settings) per project WITHOUT recompiling. Today the model is hardcoded (default_value="arctic-m" in semantic.rs:36 / embed.rs; impl Default for EmbeddingModel). No config-file machinery exists in tldr-code (no toml dep, no loader, no app Config struct).

Design (settled):
- ONE JSON file per project: .tldr/config.json. Optional global ~/.tldr/config.json.
- Precedence (deep per-field merge): CLI flag > .tldr/config.json > ~/.tldr/config.json > built-in default. Missing file/field = built-in default = today's behavior.
- Tolerant parsing: serde(default) on everything, NO deny_unknown_fields — ignore unknown keys so newer configs don't break older binaries (forward-compat).
- Schema (single embedding block, provider-discriminated so cloud slots in with no restructure):
  { version:int=1,
    embedding: { provider:"local", model:"arctic-l", endpoint?:str, api_key_env?:str, dimensions?:int },
    semantic:  { enabled:bool=true, langs?:[str] } }
- Built-in default model stays arctic-m (conservative; small/fast). Config opts up to arctic-l etc.
- Note: the semantic EMBEDDING cache is GLOBAL (~/.tldr/embeddings/cache.json) keyed by abs file_path:function:hash:model — per-project model choices coexist safely. Structural Salsa cache is per-project (.tldr/cache).
- No JSON-Schema validation file; loader validates at runtime.

This feature umbrella tracks the sub-tasks.
close_reason: All sub-tasks complete: TldrConfig structs (qw2), config loader with precedence chain (hzv), and embedder wiring with provider seam (5xn). Per-project .tldr/config.json is now supported with global ~/.tldr/config.json fallback and deep merge. CLI flag > project config > global config > built-in default. Cloud provider fails fast with clear error.

title: Install tldr diagnostic tools for supported languages
description:
tldr-code has optional diagnostic tools (doctor, diagnostics) that require external language tools to be installed. Need to run 'tldr doctor --install <language>' for each supported language in the project.

**Problem**: The current install flow does NOT mention these optional tools anywhere. Users (and agents) have no visibility into these additional capabilities, so they never get installed or used. This means diagnostic features like 'tldr doctor' and language-specific analysis are silently unavailable.

**What needs to happen**:
1. Update the install flow/documentation to include an 'Optional Diagnostic Tools' step
2. Make it clear which tools are available per language and how to install them
3. Ensure agents and users discover these capabilities during setup

Languages and their tools:
- Python: pyright, ruff, mypy
- TypeScript: typescript-language-server, tsc
- Go: gopls, golangci-lint
- Rust: rustc, cargo
- Java: checkstyle, spotbugs

Install command: tldr doctor --install <language>

Steps:
1. Run 'tldr doctor' to check current state
2. Add optional tools section to the install flow
3. Install tools for languages used in this project (Rust is primary)
4. Verify installation with 'tldr doctor' again
notes: Discovered during session — the entire workflow (CLAUDE.md, setup docs, onboarding) is missing any mention of these optional diagnostic tools. This is a gap in discoverability, not just installation.
close_reason: Updated CLAUDE.md with build commands, diagnostic tools section (tldr doctor --install), architecture overview, and conventions. INSTALL.md already had the optional tools section; SETUP.md already mentions tldr doctor. The gap was agent instructions not surfacing these capabilities.

title: DECISION: ArcticL retained as deployed default; embedding-model SIZE is not the quality lever
description: MEASURED (clean baseline, n=14, prefix ON): ArcticL (1024d) R@5 0.786/R@10 0.929/MRR 0.707 vs ArcticM (768d) R@5 0.857/R@10 0.857/MRR 0.685. The M-vs-L difference is 1-2 single-case swings = WITHIN NOISE at n=14; the models are statistically indistinguishable on this eval. DECISION: (1) RETAIN ArcticL as the deployed default (user accepts the ~3x index time / 33% larger vectors; it is a hair better @10/MRR, not worse). (2) Treat embedding-model SIZE as NOT a quality lever for code search — the bottleneck is the MODALITY GAP (English query vs wordless code), already addressed by the query prefix (12x). Future quality gains come from TLDR-4er (lexical fusion) and TLDR-9m2 (LLM descriptions), NOT a bigger encoder. (3) Do not re-chase 'use a larger embedding model' — it has been measured and it does not move recall. Revisit only with the expanded gold set (TLDR-6h3), since n=14 cannot resolve model deltas anyway.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR, 2026-06-01). Model-size measured and ruled out as a quality lever; ArcticL retained as deployed default. Recorded so future sessions don't re-chase a bigger encoder.

title: Opt-in i8 'compact mode' quantization (4x smaller index, near-lossless for cosine)
description: usearch ScalarKind::I8 quantization: ~98.9% Recall@1 (near-lossless), valid for cosine (our metric), and our embeddings are ALREADY unit-normalized (matches usearch's i8 normalize->[-127,127] process). Shrinks the on-disk/mmap'd index ~4x (1.1GB->~280MB f32->i8; b1 binary even smaller) -> faster view() load, faster exact_search, lower RSS. OPT-IN only (config 'compact'/flag): i8 reorders vs f32 so it breaks the v1 results-equivalence gate (TLDR-2a4) — must be measured against the eval harness before recommending. Reframes closed TLDR-8pt as a usearch-native option.
acceptance_criteria: A config/flag enables i8 storage; eval harness shows recall delta vs f32 within an accepted bound; index size and cold-query latency drop measurably; f32 remains default.

title: Scoped semantic queries via usearch filtered_search (path/language predicate)
description: Today 'tldr semantic <q> <subdir>' re-chunks + re-embeds the subdir each call (cold). With a single GLOBAL usearch index, usearch filtered_search_f32 + a StatefulPredicate can restrict results to a path prefix or language AT QUERY TIME — no per-scope re-embed. The metadata sidecar (key->{path,lines,lang}) supplies the predicate inputs. Big win for scoped/interactive queries once the global index + daemon warm path exist. Composable predicates (path AND language).
acceptance_criteria: tldr semantic <q> <subdir> filters a single global index by path predicate (no subdir re-embed); language filter works; results identical to filtering the unscoped results post-hoc.

title: DECISION: Arctic query prefix is ON by default (the recall fix)
description: MEASURED (eval, ArcticM, crates/tldr-core/src, 14 gold): embedding raw code without Arctic's query prefix gives near-random recall (recall@5 0.071). Prepending the trained query prefix ('Represent this sentence for searching relevant passages: ') to the QUERY only (not passages) lifts recall@5 to 0.857 / MRR to 0.685 — a 12x gain. DECISION: prefix is DEFAULT-ON (shipped e9e5427, TLDR-dlk closed). It is the model's trained-correct mode; do not regress. Caveat captured separately: it lowers absolute cosine scores -> the 0.50 CLI threshold must be recalibrated (TLDR-h27).
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: DECISION: v1 keeps f32 (exact-equivalence); i8 quantization is an opt-in 'compact mode', not default
description: usearch ScalarKind::I8 quantization is near-lossless for our case (98.9% Recall@1; valid for cosine; our embeddings are ALREADY unit-normalized — exactly usearch's i8 process) and 4x smaller (1.1GB->~280MB) + faster load/search/RSS. BUT i8 reorders results vs f32, which would break the results-equivalence PASS/FAIL gate (TLDR-2a4). DECISION: v1 ships f32 to preserve exact equivalence with the current brute-force baseline. i8 becomes an OPT-IN 'compact mode' (config/flag) AFTER v1 lands and equivalence is verified — measured against the eval harness, not assumed. (Reframes the closed TLDR-8pt as a usearch-native opt-in.)
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: Embed existing docstrings to bridge the modality gap (free, no-API-key precursor to 9m2)
description: Rust enrichment docstring field is a stub (always empty), so the 'Description:' line is omitted from embedding text vs the Python original. Populate docstrings (ast layer) for full recipe parity. Low priority; enrichment works without it.
notes: REFRAME (2026-06-01) — UPGRADED from P3, status understanding corrected. Earlier dismissed as low-value 'because enrichment is off'. WRONG framing: enrichment was gated off due to the CFG/DFG FLOW noise hurting recall — docstrings are a DIFFERENT component. Docstrings are English text the author already wrote = directly bridges the MODALITY GAP (English query vs wordless code), the root problem. So d8f is a FREE, no-API-key cousin of TLDR-9m2: embed existing docstrings to lift recall for documented functions, WITHOUT the CFG/DFG flow info. Decouple from the recall-hurting enrichment: add a docstring-only embedding-text path (gate separately from TLDR_ENRICH). Measure on the eval. Likely cheaper first step than 9m2 (which LLM-generates descriptions for functions that LACK docstrings).

title: Detection pass: annotate semantic/search code with audit findings
description: Embed the session's investigation into the code as grep-able // TLDR-AUDIT(<id>): comments so the next session can start from the code itself. Non-functional: comments only, no logic changes. Marks dead code (cs5 stub, 4er hybrid), regressions (7kf scan, k4q JSON, 8pt f32), and the misnamed flag (1a8).
close_reason: Annotated 7 files with 11 grep-able // TLDR-AUDIT(<id>): comments capturing the investigation (cs5 dead stub, 4er unwired hybrid, 7kf/k4q/8pt regressions, 1a8 misnomer, 6fm root-cause). cargo check passes, 0 errors. Comments only, no logic changes.

title: Promote semantic search to a first-class architectural layer (Layer 6: Retrieval) + document it
description: Root cause of this epic (per docs/ARCHITECTURE.md v2.0): the canonical architecture is a 5-layer STATIC stack (AST->CallGraph->CFG->DFG->PDG). Semantic/embeddings/BM25/hybrid are NOT a layer, NOT in the data-flow diagram, NOT in the caching section — they exist only as throwaway CLI lines (search.rs/semantic.rs) and one MCP wrapper. That non-adoption is WHY the subsystem rotted (FAISS-downgrade, dead HTTP stub, unwired hybrid) while the documented layers stayed clean. This issue is the umbrella architectural work that the six sibling fixes serve: elevate semantic search to a first-class layer with the same rigor as the static layers.
design: Deliverable: (a) a real Retrieval layer in tldr-core with documented key types, owning embedding + index + hybrid fusion; (b) an ARCHITECTURE.md section for Layer 6 (purpose, key types, data-flow position, caching) — the doc work has no other home; (c) the in-process usearch index as the layer's persistence/search engine. MUST honor the documented 'intentionally avoids LSP' principle: in-process only, no HTTP service, no Redis. Caching should fit the documented daemon in-memory LRU model (metadata sidecar parsed once into daemon memory).
acceptance_criteria: ARCHITECTURE.md documents Layer 6 (Semantic/Retrieval) with the same structure as Layers 1-5; semantic search is reachable as a coherent tldr-core module rather than scattered CLI/MCP glue; the 6 sibling fixes (7kf/8pt/k4q/4er/cs5/1a8) land under this layer.
notes: SESSION UPDATE (2026-06-01): Scope grew. 'Layer 6: Retrieval' documentation must now also cover the mmap persistence engine from epic TLDR-ac0 (the decided storage layer) and the query-prefix recipe (TLDR-dlk). The Layer-6 ARCHITECTURE.md section should describe: chunker -> embedder (with Arctic query/passage prefix) -> mmap vector store -> brute-force top_k -> optional RRF hybrid (4er). Best done LAST, after 4er/ac0 land so the doc matches reality.

title: EmbeddingClient HTTP path is a no-op stub
description: embedding_client.rs:197 .search() returns Ok(Vec::new()) — the HTTP embedding service path does nothing and silently returns empty. FIX: implement the HTTP call, or delete the path entirely. Currently a trap for anyone who wires it in expecting results.
notes: SESSION UPDATE (2026-06-01): Still accurate — EmbeddingClient HTTP path is a no-op stub. Now has concrete urgency: it is the blocker for TLDR-4er, whose value is no longer hypothetical (dense-alone misses 2/14 lexically-strong gold cases that BM25 fusion should recover). Fix = delete the stub + repoint 4er's semantic side at in-process SemanticIndex. Per epic NOTES, the 8765 HTTP service this stub implied NEVER existed in either codebase.
close_reason: Dead EmbeddingClient HTTP stub DELETED (search/embedding_client.rs + types.rs placeholder + mod/lib re-exports + tests). hybrid_search decoupled from any embedding backend — now takes &[SemanticResult] supplied by the caller. Full workspace builds green.

title: RRF hybrid fusion is dead code (unwired)
description: search/hybrid.rs implements BM25+dense Reciprocal Rank Fusion but is referenced NOWHERE in the live CLI (grep confirms zero hits outside archived/). Production gives EITHER lexical (tldr search/BM25) OR dense (tldr semantic), never fused. FIX: wire enriched_search / tldr semantic to call fuse_rrf so the hybrid path actually runs.
design: This is the highest-value item: the most modern retrieval idea in the repo never executes.
notes: SESSION UPDATE (2026-06-01): Concrete value evidence now exists. Dense-alone (prefix ON) misses exactly 2/14 gold cases — 'extract a control flow graph from a function' (cfg/extractor.rs) and 'build the enriched text...' (enrichment.rs) — both LEXICALLY strong (a fn literally named extract_cfg*), precisely where BM25 fusion should recover them. So 4er is not just 'completeness'; it has a measurable recall target (could lift 0.857 toward 1.0). IMPL SKETCH (read this session): repoint hybrid_search's semantic side from the dead EmbeddingClient (TLDR-cs5) to the in-process SemanticIndex; aggregate chunk-level dense hits to FILE granularity (BM25 Bm25Result keys by file, RRF fuses on file path string); handle #[cfg(feature=semantic)] gating (hybrid.rs is currently un-gated in search/); wire hybrid_search into the CLI (today it is called nowhere outside the crate). Blocked on cs5.
close_reason: RRF hybrid fusion WIRED end-to-end. hybrid_search decoupled from the dead stub (cs5); new hybrid_search_with_index builds dense from in-process SemanticIndex, reduces to best-per-file, RRF-fuses with BM25. Wired into BOTH consumers: 'tldr semantic --hybrid' CLI + MCP tldr_semantic tool (was BM25-only). Fixed a path-key bug (dense=cwd-relative vs BM25=root-relative -> overlap always 0; now strip root so fusion actually combines). MEASURED (ArcticL, prefix ON, n=14): dense->hybrid Recall@5 0.786->0.929, Recall@10 0.929->1.000, MRR 0.718->0.783; cfg/extractor.rs recovered (— -> rank 8). Eval measures the exact production path. Caveat: file-level fusion; n=14 (see 6h3). Full build green, 31/31 search tests pass.

title: Vector search uses brute-force scalar scan (no SIMD/ANN)
description: similarity.rs:237 does an O(n) linear scan with a plain scalar cosine loop on every query. Defensible at the 10K-100K target scale (sub-ms, exact), but leaves a 5-10x constant-factor win on the table and the 100K ceiling reaches ~10ms/query. REPLACE: SimSIMD kernel dropped into top_k_similar for the cheap win; usearch (HNSW) only if scale ever exceeds ~100K vectors.
design: Swap surface is exactly one function: top_k_similar in semantic/similarity.rs. No design change needed for SimSIMD; usearch would require an index build/persist step.
notes: SESSION/DESIGN UPDATE (2026-06-01): CHALLENGED BY MEASUREMENT (epic TLDR-ac0). ac0 measured the cold-query breakdown: 4300ms JSON parse + 500ms model load + 200ms query embed + 0.1ms brute-force cosine. The scalar scan THIS issue targets is the 0.1ms term — NOT the bottleneck. ac0 keeps exact brute-force (HNSW/usearch is approximate AND only saves ~0.1ms->0.5ms). Likely SUPERSEDED by ac0 once the mmap-vs-usearch fork (TLDR-rgc) is locked. Do NOT implement SIMD/ANN until rgc decides. The old handoff framing 'usearch subsumes 7kf' is itself stale — ac0 argues AGAINST usearch.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by usearch decision (TLDR-rgc, 2026-06-01). usearch exact_search_f32 owns the vector scan — exact, SIMD-accelerated in C++. Also moot per ac0 measurement (scan was 0.1ms, never the bottleneck). No custom SIMD/ANN needed.

title: Semantic search: modernize vector lookup + complete hybrid retrieval
description: Umbrella for improving tldr's semantic/search subsystem. Two themes: (1) the vector-lookup mechanics are naive (brute-force scalar scan, f32, JSON persistence) though defensible at current scale; (2) the most modern retrieval design in the tree (RRF hybrid fusion) is built but unwired, and an alternate embedding path is a stub. Children track each problem and replacement. We will pick each child and discuss before implementing.
design:
FRAMING: Perf items (scan/quant/persist) are REGRESSIONS from the Python original (FAISS), not net-new gaps. Completeness items (dead RRF path, stub client, misnamed flag) are the higher-value fixes. The rewrite as a whole was net-positive (better modular architecture, in-process Arctic embeddings); it only regressed the vector-INDEX layer and left two features half-built.

DECIDED DIRECTION: Finish the in-process path with usearch (the Rust FAISS-equivalent that closes the FAISS-shaped hole WITHOUT a service dependency). Do NOT revive the HTTP embedding service (re-introduces cross-language/operational fragility the author was rightly avoiding). Do NOT add Redis (external server; the daemon already provides warm in-memory). 

usearch SUBSUMES three children: its Rust binding (usearch/rust/lib.rs) provides save/load + view (mmap) + ScalarKind quantization (F32/BF16/I8/B1x8) natively. So 7kf (scan), 8pt (quant), k4q (persistence) collapse into 'adopt usearch' + one small residual task: a metadata sidecar (u64 key -> {path,lines,snippet,hash,mtime}) that usearch does NOT store. JSON sidecar is acceptable — daemon parses it once and holds it in memory, so format has zero query-time cost.
notes:
PYTHON-PRECEDENT (llm-tldr/tldr/semantic.py): Python was MORE sophisticated at vector search — in-process BGE-large-en-v1.5 (sentence-transformers, 1024d) + FAISS IndexFlatIP + binary .faiss persistence (semantic.py:1068,1072,1134). Rust kept in-process embeddings (good, Arctic 768d) but DOWNGRADED the index: FAISS->scalar loop, binary->JSON. So 7kf/k4q are regressions, not net-new.

THE 8765 SERVICE NEVER EXISTED — ANYWHERE. Grep of the Python repo: no FastAPI/Flask/uvicorn/8765/endpoints. Python embedded in-process (semantic.py:206). So embedding_client.rs (cs5) was a NEW planned architecture, not a wrap of existing Python. The BGE-1024 model ref shows it was meant to run the same engine as Python, as a separate service.

AUTHOR DESIGN INTENT (reconstructed): strangler-fig migration — defer the hard ML port (sentence-transformers+FAISS are hard in Rust) by keeping embeddings in a Python HTTP service and calling it from Rust during the transition. The 'Mitigation M8 graceful-degradation -> BM25-only' header comment proves they knew the dependency was fragile. Then they pivoted to in-process fastembed (correct call) but only finished HALF: embeddings came in-process, FAISS's job was NOT replaced (no Rust ANN lib reached for) -> hand-rolled scalar scan. Result: dead HTTP stub + regressed index. usearch is the library that finishes the pivot.

8765 IS NOT LSP AND NOT MCP — it is a 4th orphaned subsystem. Transports proven: daemon=Unix socket / TCP axum (port=hash-of-project, NOT 8765); MCP=stdio JSON-RPC (no port); diagnostics 'LSP' tools=subprocess+parse stdout (NOT real LSP sessions; only severity enum is 'LSP-compatible'); embedding_client=HTTP REST (dead). NOTE: MCP is a CONSUMER of semantic — tldr_semantic is an exposed MCP tool (docs/MCP.md:107) — so the dead embedding layer degrades an agent-facing capability that is already wired up. That raises the value of completing 4er.

ROOT CAUSE — SEMANTIC SEARCH WAS NEVER ARCHITECTURALLY ADOPTED (docs/ARCHITECTURE.md v2.0). The canonical architecture is a 5-layer STATIC stack: AST -> Call Graph -> CFG -> DFG -> PDG. Semantic/embeddings/BM25/hybrid are NOT a layer, NOT in the data-flow diagram, NOT in the caching section — they appear only as two throwaway CLI lines (search.rs/semantic.rs) and one MCP wrapper. This explains WHY the subsystem rotted: the 5 documented layers stayed clean/tested while search — never first-class — got FAISS-downgraded, left with a dead stub and an unwired hybrid path. REFRAME: this epic is not just bug-cleanup; it is PROMOTING semantic search to a first-class architectural layer (Layer 6: Semantic/Retrieval) with the same rigor as the static layers. Deliverable should include: (a) a real Retrieval layer in tldr-core, (b) an ARCHITECTURE.md section documenting it (layer purpose, key types, data-flow, caching), (c) the in-process usearch index as its persistence/search engine.

DESIGN-PRINCIPLE FIT: ARCHITECTURE.md states 'intentionally avoids LSP' for speed/token-efficiency. usearch (in-process, no server) HONORS that principle; reviving the HTTP service or adding Redis would VIOLATE it. The documented caching model (daemon in-memory LRU keyed by tool+args+mtimes) also matches the proposed JSON metadata sidecar (parsed once into daemon memory) — so the recommended direction is consistent with the intended architecture, not a deviation.

MODEL NOTE: Python used BGE-large (1024d); Rust uses Arctic (768d). Deliberate swap (Arctic newer), but index dimensions differ from the Python era — not a parity port.

DOC DRIFT: ARCHITECTURE.md says 18 languages (overview) but the tier table lists 17; CLAUDE.md context implies ~15. Language count not pinned consistently — sign the docs are loosely maintained.

title: Tests + docs for usearch store migration
description: Update cache.rs unit tests for the new store (open/get/contains/add/flush, corruption, version mismatch, concurrent access). Update CLAUDE.md / build notes: confirm NO new C++ toolchain dependency (pure-Rust path), document the per-model store files and the GC/compaction command. Ensure cargo test -p tldr-core --features semantic and tldr-cli semantic tests pass (note: pre-existing PoisonError flakiness in tldr-cli semantic tests is tracked separately).
notes: REFRAMED mmap->usearch.

title: Migrate/discard cache.json -> usearch index
description: Handle the existing 1.1GB cache.json (~/Library/Caches/tldr/embeddings/cache.json, 117,641 entries) gracefully -- must NOT crash on first run with old cache present (smoke test #9). Options: (a) one-time import that reads cache.json once and writes per-model store files, then deletes/renames the JSON; (b) ignore old cache and lazily rebuild on demand (slower first runs but simplest). Decide and implement; ensure no data-loss surprise for users mid-upgrade.
notes: REFRAMED. The 1.1GB cache.json is already dead (raw-v1 key bump orphaned the 117K old entries). Simplest path: drop it, rebuild the usearch index from chunks on next run (re-embed once). No row-by-row migration needed unless we want to preserve current raw-v1 vectors.

title: GC-by-reachability for orphaned vectors
description: On a full-root re-index, enumerate all live keys (from chunk_code over the whole project) and drop store entries not re-added this pass -- reclaims orphans from deleted/renamed/changed code. Single-file embeds can't see the whole project, so orphans accumulate until a full pass; expose a compaction trigger (e.g. tldr embed --gc or tldr index --compact). Content-hash keys already guarantee correctness, so this is space reclamation only.
notes: REFRAMED: GC = rebuild the usearch index from live chunks on full re-index (per TLDR-rgc GC decision). Orphaned vectors simply don't get re-added. Drops per-entry timestamps; matches the rebuild model.

title: 'tldr search --hybrid' flag is misnamed (BM25+regex, not dense fusion)
description: search.rs:88 SearchMode::Hybrid means 'BM25 + regex pattern filter', NOT dense+lexical hybrid. Misleading given a real RRF hybrid exists in the tree. FIX: rename the flag (e.g. --filter), or repoint it at the real RRF hybrid once wired.
close_reason: Renamed misleading CLI flag --hybrid -> --filter (it is BM25 + regex post-filter, NOT lexical+dense fusion). --hybrid reserved for real RRF fusion (TLDR-4er). search.rs.

title: Embedding cache persisted as JSON (ASCII floats)
description: cache.rs serializes embeddings as JSON text; the whole file is deserialized into RAM each run. REPLACE: binary format (bincode/rkyv) or memory-mapped file. Faster load, smaller disk, no parse cost.
notes: SESSION/DESIGN UPDATE (2026-06-01): SUBSUMED by epic TLDR-ac0 — replacing the JSON cache (1.1GB ASCII floats, 4.3s parse) IS the whole point of ac0 (mmap store). Superseded by ac0 children TLDR-m01 (rewire EmbeddingCache onto MmapVectorStore) + TLDR-3gj (migrate cache.json). Keep open only as a pointer until ac0 lands. CONFIRMED this session: the e9e5427 cache-key bump (raw-v1 schema tag) orphaned the old 117K model-only-keyed entries -> ~1.1GB DEAD weight now sitting in cache.json, sharpening the urgency.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by usearch decision (TLDR-rgc). usearch save/load + view (binary mmap) replaces the JSON cache — kills the 4.3s parse. Old 1.1GB cache.json is dead weight, discarded on migration (TLDR-3gj).

title: Embeddings stored as full f32, no quantization
description: EmbeddedChunk stores Vec<f32> (~3KB/vector, 357MB at the 100K cap; types.rs:73). REPLACE: scalar/int8 quantization (or usearch built-in i8/b1) to cut memory 2-8x and speed the scan.
notes: SESSION/DESIGN UPDATE (2026-06-01): DEPRIORITIZED by epic TLDR-ac0. ac0 keeps f32 — quantization (f16/i8) reorders brute-force results, and the cold-query cost is the cache LOAD (4.3s) not vector width. Likely superseded/deferred once TLDR-rgc locks the backend. Don't quantize unless a measured memory/size problem reopens it.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by usearch decision (TLDR-rgc). Quantization is built into usearch via ScalarKind (add_f32/f16/i8/b1x8) — opt-in if a size problem ever arises. No custom quant code needed.

title: [Future] Cloud embedding provider behind config seam
description: NOT now — placeholder so the seam is tracked. When implementing cloud embeddings: introduce an EmbeddingProvider trait (embed_batch) with FastEmbedProvider (local, current Embedder) and a RemoteProvider impl. The existing search/embedding_client.rs is a NON-FUNCTIONAL stub (HTTP body unimplemented — comments say 'would use reqwest'; second placeholder unit-struct EmbeddingClient in types.rs:2938; not reachable from CLI). Reuse its request/response shapes + graceful-degradation idea (M8 -> BM25 fallback). Work: add reqwest, real POST to endpoint, auth header from env var named by api_key_env (never store the key), carry dimensions from config (cloud dims differ, e.g. OpenAI 3-large=3072; cache/index need model->dim). Cache already keys by model so cloud embeddings cache cleanly. Flag: cloud means sending code to a third party (privacy). Depends on the config seam landing first.
